PG5.template: This is what (insert thing) looks like when you're (insert action). And this is what they look like when you're not (insert action).
PG5.text: This is what a card looks like to the buyer. This is what it looks like to the supplier.

PG5.template: More (insert target audience) need to hear this, (insert common belief) will not (insert result).
PG5.text: More business owners need to hear this. The card isn't bad, it's just not for this.

PG5.template: Here's exactly how much (insert action/item) you need to (insert result)
PG5.text: Here's exactly who pays the 2.95% card fee, and it isn't the business owner.

PG5.template: (insert item) (insert fact), (insert complete opposite item) (insert similar fact).
PG5.text: The buyer sees a payment method, the supplier sees 180 days of liability.

PG5.template: Everyone tells you to (insert action) but nobody actually tells you how to do it. Here is a #-second step-by-step tutorial that you can save.
PG5.text: Everyone talks about card fees, nobody explains who actually pays them.

PG5.template: Here's what every (audience) needs to know about (topic).
PG5.text: Here's what every business owner needs to know about who really pays the card fee on China orders.

Here's the thing nobody tells you. You're looking at the credit card like a way to pay. Your supplier is looking at it like a risk to their family business. Let me show you both sides at the same time.
